sections.paragraph:
to put into motion, to disturb; kindred to the roots הָמַם, הָמָה. Deu 7:23, הָמָם מְהוּמָה גְדֹולָה “he brings upon them great disturbance.” Whence תְּהֹום pr. the sea in commotion.

Niphal, fut. יֵהֹם to be disturbed, to be in commotion, as a city, land, Rth 1:19 1Sa 4:5 1Ki 1:45.

Hiphil, to make a commotion, Lermen machen, toben (comp. הִשְׁקִיט Ruhe halten), used of a multitude making a noise, Mic 2:12 of an uneasy mind (Toben im Jnnern), Psa 55:3.

Derivatives, מְהוּמָה, תְּהֹום.
